raw.summary: The code meets most functional requirements but has hardcoded credentials (admin password and CSRF token) as reported by SAST. These are true positives because the values are static literals in the source code, which violates security best practices for authentication and CSRF protection. Additionally, the authentication method uses a non-standard base64 decode that will fail at runtime, and the /status page reads an arbitrary environment variable from the query string without validation, exposing any environment variable. However, in review_only mode, only the SAST findings are formally reviewed.
